title: platform-user — developer configuration
description: Runtime configuration for the platform-user module. Every key here is either an operational tuning parameter (SLAs, retention windows, MFA enforcement) or an integration credential (PagerDuty for on-call rotation). Nothing under this module is tenant-facing — there is no per-tenant override via the settings module. Every credential resolves via env only (Doppler-managed in staging + prod).

onboarding.description: Onboarding checklist reminder policy. New PlatformUsers land in status=`pending`; the module tracks time-since-hire and pings the manager + the new hire on a schedule until they promote to active.

keys.description: Days-since-hire on which to remind. After the last day, escalates to the department head.
Days-since-hire at which a pending PlatformUser triggers an escalation notification to super_admin + ops.
When the onboarding checklist reports 100% complete, automatically transition status=pending→active. Off = super_admin must explicitly activate via the platform-admin UI.

offboarding.description: Offboarding SLA + retention hold policy. The 24h SLA is a SOC 2 CC6.7 control — access to systems must be revoked promptly after termination. The 90-day retention hold is a compliance-audit affordance.

keys.description: Target completion window for RevokePlatformUserAccessJob. Alerting fires at 12h (half-SLA).
Grace window between PlatformUserOffboarded event and access-revocation job start. Allows a planned offboard to be reversed within the same day (rare but supported).
Cap on effective_at future-dating. Prevents an actor from queueing offboardings months in advance.

retention.description: Retention hold on offboarded PlatformUser rows. During the hold, the row is soft-deleted but not hard-deleted — audit rows written before offboard still resolve to the display_name. Post-hold, PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob hard-deletes.

keys.description: Days between soft-delete (offboard) and hard-delete. 90 days matches the compliance-audit lookup window used by SOC 2 attestations.

When true (default), audit rows referencing this PlatformUser are RETAINED after hard delete with an anonymised actor placeholder. When false, audit rows are anonymised via a hash of the original user_id at hard-delete time. NEVER set to false without a compliance-officer sign-off — SOC2 evidence retention requires the former.

mfa.description: MFA enforcement on the platform_admin guard. Stricter than the tenant plane — platform staff have zero option to opt out of MFA.

keys.description: Refuse hiring an Identity that has NULL mfa_secret_encrypted. Off = allow (used only in dev).
The `platform.mfa_required` middleware enforces this at request time: refuses tokens issued to Identities that have since disabled MFA. Off = only checked at login (weaker; not recommended).
Sanctum PAT TTL on the platform_admin guard. Shorter than the tenant plane's 8h default — platform sessions time out at 1h so an unlocked laptop is exposed for less time.

on_call.description: On-call rotation resolver settings. Off by default; enable per-environment via PLATFORM_USER_ON_CALL_ENABLED. When enabled, primary backend is PagerDuty; fallback is the internal on_call_rotation_entries table (seeded via `data/on-call-departments.json`).

keys.description: Master toggle. Off = OnCallResolver returns null for every query + no PagerDuty API calls fire.
Which resolver backs OnCallResolver by default. `internal` = read from on_call_rotation_entries table. `pagerduty` = call PagerDuty's schedules API.
PagerDuty API HTTP timeout. Short because on-call resolution is on a critical path (production incident routing).
OnCallResolver result cache TTL. Short so a rotation change (someone offboarded mid-shift) propagates within a minute.

When true (default in staging + prod), every mutating Artisan command must be invoked with --reason=<text>. Off in dev; on in staging + prod (SOC2 evidence). Audit rows for CLI-driven mutations carry the reason string in the tags field.

audit.description: Audit stamping policy specific to the platform plane.

keys.description: When true, the `platform.audit` middleware writes a shared correlation_id to every audit + activity row in the same request. Consumed by the `platform-user:audit-report` command to group related mutations.
Hot-storage window for platform-plane audit rows. Longer than the tenant plane's default (matches SOC 2 attestation window). Post-hot, rows rotate to cold via audit::RotateAuditColdStorageJob.
